name: Alabama Department of Rehabilitation Services (ADRS)
description: State agency providing services for children and adults with disabilities including early intervention, vocational rehabilitation, and independent living services.

name: Alabama Early Intervention System (AEIS)
description: Part C early intervention program providing developmental services for infants and toddlers birth to age 3 with developmental delays or disabilities.

name: The Arc of Alabama
description: Statewide advocacy organization for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, offering resources, support, and community programs.

name: Special Olympics Alabama
description: Year-round sports training and athletic competition for children and adults with intellectual disabilities across Alabama.

name: Alabama Disability Rights (Alabama Disabilities Advocacy Program)
description: Federally mandated protection and advocacy system providing legal advocacy for Alabamians with disabilities.

name: Alabama Respite Network
description: Respite care referral network helping families of children and adults with disabilities find temporary relief care services.

name: Camp ASCCA
description: Alabama's Special Camp for Children and Adults, offering year-round programs and summer camp for individuals with disabilities.
address: 5278 Camp ASCCA Dr, Jackson Gap, AL 36861

name: STAR (Statewide Technology Access and Response) for Alabamians with Disabilities
description: Assistive technology program providing device demonstrations, short-term loans, and assistance accessing assistive technology for Alabamians with disabilities.

name: Alabama ABLE Savings Plan
description: Tax-advantaged savings accounts for individuals with disabilities to save without affecting eligibility for public benefits like Medicaid and SSI.

name: Alabama Medicaid Waiver Programs
description: Home and community-based Medicaid waiver programs providing services for individuals with intellectual disabilities and other conditions to remain in their communities.

name: ClasTran Paratransit (Birmingham)
description: Paratransit and demand-response transportation service for individuals with disabilities in the Birmingham metro area.

name: Alabama Housing Finance Authority - Accessible Housing
description: State housing authority providing information on accessible and affordable housing options for individuals with disabilities.

name: UAB Developmental Pediatrics
description: Developmental and behavioral pediatrics program at the University of Alabama at Birmingham providing comprehensive evaluations and treatment for children with developmental concerns.

name: Easter Seals of Central Alabama Pediatric Therapy
description: Pediatric therapy services including physical, occupational, and speech therapy for children with disabilities.

name: Alabama Vocational Rehabilitation Service - Employment
description: Employment services including job training, placement, and support for individuals with disabilities seeking competitive integrated employment.

name: Lakeshore Foundation Adaptive Recreation
description: Adaptive sports and recreation programs for individuals with physical disabilities including wheelchair sports, fitness, and wellness programs.

name: Lakeshore Foundation
description: World leader in adaptive fitness and athletic performance, serving as the National Governing Body for USA Wheelchair Rugby. Offers 90+ weekly classes and competitive adaptive sports programs.

name: The Arc of Central Alabama
description: Local Arc chapter serving the Birmingham area, providing lifelong support services for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: The Arc of Madison County
description: Local Arc chapter championing advocacy, acceptance, and inclusion for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, including group homes, early childhood programs, and an autism clinic.
